name: Chocolate Nutmeg Cakeroll
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cups Nestle Toll House Semi-sweet Chocolate Morsels (reserved)
recipeInstructions:
Chocolate Sponge Cake: One 12 oz. package (2 cups) Nestle Toll House Semi-sweet Chocolate Morsels - divided
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Melt half cup morsels over hot (not boiling) water; stir until smooth. Set aside.
3. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.
4. Beat egg yolks and vanilla together for about 5 minutes until thick.
5. Gradually add 1/4 cup sugar, beating until sugar dissolves.
6. Gradually add melted morsels, beating well.
7. Beat egg whites until soft peaks form.
8. Gradually add remaining 1/4 cup sugar; beat until stiff peaks form.
9. Fold in chocolate mixture.
10. Sprinkle with flour mixture and fold in.
11. Spread evenly in a greased and floured 15 x 10 x 1-inch pan.
12. Bake at 375°F for 12 to 15 minutes.
13. Loosen cake; invert onto a towel sprinkled with confectioner's sugar.
14. Roll up the cake starting from the short side, using the towel.
15. Cool seam-side down.
16. Unroll and spread evenly with filling.
17. Roll up without the towel and sprinkle with confectioner's sugar.
18. Top with chocolate leaves.
Chocolate Nutmeg Filling:
1. In a blender container, combine 3 tablespoons cold water and the gelatin; let stand for 2 minutes.
2. Add one-third cup boiling water. Cover and blend on high speed until gelatin dissolves.
3. Add 1 1/4 cups morsels, sugar, vanilla, and nutmeg. Cover and blend until smooth.
4. With the blender on low speed, add the egg yolk with cream.
5. Add half cup ice cubes (about 3); blend until mixture begins to thicken and ice cubes melt.
6. Transfer to a bowl; place in an ice bath until mounded (about 15 minutes).
Chocolate Leaves:
1. Melt remaining 1/4 cup morsels over hot (not boiling) water.
2. With a small spatula, coat the underside of a dry mint leaf or silk leaf with chocolate.
3. Wipe off the front of the leaf.
4. Place on waxed paper lined cookie sheet; freeze until firm.
5. Peel the leaf off to reveal the chocolate leaf.
